title: Wylie Avenue Days
rights_note: Do not republish the full PBS transcript inside the public app. Use this structured metadata, extracted entities, theme bins, and short evidence notes for corpus indexing and human verification.
corpus_use: Adds Pittsburgh Hill District oral-history and documentary evidence to the Sanborn Research LLM corpus. Useful for entity extraction, corridor analysis, displacement/urban renewal framing, social infrastructure modeling, and candidate node verification.
summary: The documentary presents Wylie Avenue and the Hill District as a dense Black cultural, commercial, social, religious, nightlife, newspaper, sports, and political ecosystem. It describes the neighborhood's rise during the Great Migration, its internal institutions and businesses, the role of segregation in shaping parallel Black institutions, the importance of Wylie Avenue and Fullerton Street as a crossroads, and the later damage caused by urban renewal, Civic Arena construction, displacement, and the post-1968 decline of the lower Hill.

gis_handoff_notes: Prioritize geocoding the Fullerton/Wylie crossroads cluster and Crawford Street / Freedom Corner boundary.
Separate local corridor nodes from intercity performer/network evidence.
Treat Civic Arena/Lower Hill redevelopment as a displacement polygon or impact zone rather than a single point.
Use the transcript as evidence for Pittsburgh node selection and social-infrastructure categories, not as final address verification by itself.
